name: fd-manufacturing-disposition-taxonomy
focus: Map MES/MRB/CAPA nonconformance disposition workflows onto AI agent output failures
persona: Industrial quality systems engineer with ISO 9001 nonconformance management and MRB experience.
decision_lens: Every nonconforming output needs disposition with documented rationale and authority signature.
review_areas: MRB authority structures: who dispositions scrap vs rework vs use-as-is and evidence thresholds
CAPA triggers: when single nonconformance escalates to systemic process change vs one-off
Nonconformance cost accounting in MES: rework labor, scrap write-off, quarantine holding, deviation overhead
Quarantine workflows: logical isolation, hold tags, time limits, escalation when quarantined items age out
Use-as-is and deviation: formal engineering judgment for out-of-spec items meeting functional requirements
First-pass yield vs rework yield metrics driving process improvement
success_hints: MRB decision criteria distinguishing rework-is-cost-effective from scrap-and-restart
CAPA escalation trigger (single vs pattern) mapping to process change vs re-run
task_context: AI factory needs formal disposition taxonomy for agent output failures beyond reopen-ticket. MES/MRB/CAPA workflows have solved this.

name: fd-cicd-failure-modes
focus: Catalog CI/CD failure taxonomies and existing disposition strategies
persona: Senior platform engineer with reliability pipeline experience at scale.
decision_lens: Software delivery has implicit disposition decisions already — formalize into explicit taxonomy matching manufacturing rigor.
review_areas: Flaky test disposition: quarantine buckets, deflake SLAs, known-bad registries
Partial deployment failures: blue-green rollback, canary abort, forward-fix decisions
Build artifact promotion gates and implicit use-as-is decisions
CI failure triage in large orgs (Google TAP, Meta Sandcastle)
Rollback cost modeling: when rollback is cheap vs costly
Feature flags as runtime deviations with audit trails
success_hints: Flaky test quarantine as analog to manufacturing quarantine
Forward-fix vs rollback criteria mapping to rework vs scrap
Artifact promotion models approximating use-as-is disposition
task_context: Demarch beads flow through agent pipelines with CUJ gates. Extract CI/CD disposition patterns and map to formal taxonomy.

name: fd-film-vfx-revision-pipelines
focus: Extract revision approval workflow patterns from film/VFX dailies pipelines
persona: VFX production supervisor with Shotgrid/Flow Production Tracking experience.
decision_lens: VFX revision pipelines are mature multi-stakeholder approval where partial work ships under conditional approval.
review_areas: Dailies review structure: submission to supervisor to client, disposition states at each gate
Conditional approval: approved-pending-fix as formal state, downstream work proceeding while fix tracked
Rework loop: structured notes, immediate rework vs notes-pending queue
Omit/cut disposition: VFX equivalent of scrap, cost implications, tracking
Parallel streams at different quality levels with conditional approvals
Escalation when shot consistently fails review: reassign, redesign, or cut
success_hints: Dailies cost-of-revision tracking driving omit decisions
Shotgrid status schema mapped to rework taxonomy
task_context: AI agent output has structural similarities to VFX shots — partial completion common, multi-stakeholder review required, rework cost varies.

name: fd-cuj-rework-integration
focus: How three-state CUJ gate (pass/marginal/fail) triggers and constrains rework disposition
persona: Quality systems architect thinking in signal tiers, threshold calibration, and decision theory.
decision_lens: Marginal state is the most information-rich and dangerous zone — disposition must be sensitive to which tier triggered and what evidence exists.
review_areas: Marginal state semantics: usable vs risky, different disposition paths
Signal tier × disposition authority interaction
CUJ gate output as nonconformance evidence populating disposition record
Rework verification: full CUJ re-run vs only failed gates, risks of each
Marginal accumulation: when pattern triggers CAPA-equivalent systemic review
Quarantine-to-disposition SLA: time limits, escalation responsibility
success_hints: Three-state quality systems with formal logic for middle state
Rework verification full vs targeted cost tradeoff
Signal tier × disposition authority as 2D design matrix
task_context: Demarch has CUJ gates with three states and signal tiers. Design formal mapping from gate output to rework taxonomy disposition.

name: fd-rework-cost-accounting
focus: Cost economics of each disposition path using interstat token and time data
persona: Engineering economist applying cost-of-quality frameworks to AI agent systems.
decision_lens: Every disposition is implicitly a cost decision — scrap trades rework overhead for fresh-start quality; quarantine trades decision cost against holding cost.
review_areas: Cost-of-quality framework: prevention, appraisal, internal failure, external failure costs
Scrap vs rework break-even: manufacturing formula adapted for token costs
Quarantine holding cost: blocked downstream work, decision overhead, eventual disposition
Deviation downstream defect cost: probability of failure × cost of failure vs certain rework cost
Interstat schema extension for rework tracking: cost signals per disposition event
Second-order costs of poor disposition decisions and authority controls
success_hints: COQ literature formulas translating to token costs
Interstat schema extension for disposition-tagged costs
Quarantine holding cost / decision deferral literature
task_context: Demarch tracks costs via interstat. Build economic model justifying disposition path choice using actual token and time data.

name: fd-authority-rework-routing
focus: Authority tier decision model for disposition routing — who authorizes scrap vs rework vs use-as-is
persona: Organizational systems designer studying decision rights frameworks in engineering quality management.
decision_lens: Disposition authority calibrated to cost and reversibility — agent self-authorizes cheap rework, escalates scrap or use-as-is.
review_areas: Map Demarch authority tiers to disposition types, identify gaps
MRB authority scoping by severity, cost threshold, safety criticality
Self-disposition vs escalation triggers: when agent dispositions own output
Deviation authority: permanent spec-level change transcending individual work item
Quarantine as authority limbo: escalation path resolving before permanent
Audit trail for disposition decisions supporting retrospective analysis
success_hints: Whether disposition authority inherits from domain authority or is separate
Deviation authority analog: only engineering authorizes spec changes
Quarantine-to-escalation time-bound trigger with clear authority owner
task_context: Demarch authority tier model exists for domain-scoped decisions. Extend with rework-specific disposition authority matrix.
